commands.repair_reason: No repair lane is needed: the previous narrow parser defect is addressed in the latest commit, and the remaining action is maintainer draft/validation handling.; Cleared: The diff tightens CLI parsing and config diagnostics without adding dependencies, workflows, permissions, secret precedence changes, or new network/code-execution paths. commands.review_summary:
The PR tightens CLI/config validation and diagnostics for configure, setup, includes/env substitution, channels logs, agent timeout, sessions active filters, and model scan count options, with focused tests and changelog entries.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of current main shows permissive `parseInt`, truthy fallback, and fallback-to-all paths for the affected CLI/config inputs; I did not execute commands because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No repair lane is needed: the previous narrow parser defect is addressed in the latest commit, and the remaining action is maintainer draft/validation handling.

target.title: fix(cli): harden config and command validation
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: several operator-facing CLI/config paths silently fell back, partially parsed bad numeric input, or hid the useful config parse detail.
- Why it matters: setup, logs, session filters, agent sends, and model scans should fail before doing the wrong thing.
- What changed: stricter command validation plus clearer setup/include/env diagnostics, with focused regressions for each path.
- What did NOT change: no auth boundary changes, no credential precedence changes, no config migration behavior changes, and no public config key semantics changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes: none.
- Supersedes: none found. Live searches found no open PRs for `configure --section`, `channels logs`, `models scan`, `session active`, or `agent timeout`.
- Related: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/16351, specifically the invalid-config/validation slice.
- Related: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/74575, broad configuration simplification.
- Related: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/50274, adjacent model/config guardrail work.
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: bad operator inputs now fail explicitly instead of falling back or partially parsing; invalid config setup blockers now include useful issue detail.
- Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox on branch `impact/config-command-operator-flow`, head `cd39018c61f041aaa0d14b6ea3af03f4f3709d9a`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: reset the Testbox checkout to `origin/impact/config-command-operator-flow`, fetched/deepened `origin/main`, then ran `pnpm test:serial src/commands/configure.commands.test.ts src/config/includes.test.ts src/commands/onboard-non-interactive.invalid-config.test.ts src/config/env-substitution.test.ts src/commands/channels.logs.test.ts src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts src/commands/sessions.test.ts src/commands/models/scan.test.ts && pnpm check:changed`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Testbox `tbx_01krwcxengn7r9cnadeq0fh4vs`, Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26009518054. Output showed `remote_sha=cd39018c61f041aaa0d14b6ea3af03f4f3709d9a`, `changed_count=17`, 122 targeted tests passed, and `pnpm check:changed` exited 0 with lanes `core, coreTests, docs`.
- Observed result after fix: targeted command/config regression tests passed and changed gate passed on the exact branch head.
- What was not tested: live provider/channel services; this change is CLI parser and config diagnostic behavior covered by unit regressions plus changed gate.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): existing code used `parseInt`/fallback behavior that accepted junk suffixes or silently broadened/defaulted filters.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: command handlers accepted permissive parsing paths (`parseInt`, numeric truncation, fallback-to-all/defaults) and config errors dropped useful parse/path context.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no focused regressions for invalid `--section`, bad log filters/limits, malformed time/count options, root env substitution labels, included JSON parse details, or invalid non-interactive setup diagnostics.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/commands/*.test.ts` and `src/config/*.test.ts` touched in this PR.
- Scenario the test should lock in: invalid operator inputs fail with actionable messages and do not continue into broader/default behavior.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: these are parser/diagnostic branches with no live service dependency.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `openclaw configure --section typo` now errors before opening the full wizard.
- Non-interactive setup blocked by invalid config now includes config issue details.
- Invalid included JSON now reports parser detail in the include error.
- Root-level `${VAR}` substitution failures now show `<root>` instead of a blank config path.
- `openclaw channels logs --channel typo` now errors instead of showing all logs.
- `openclaw channels logs --lines wat` and `--lines 2.5` now error instead of defaulting/truncating.
- `openclaw agent --timeout 10wat` and `--timeout 1.5` now error instead of partially parsing.
- `openclaw sessions --active 10wat` now errors instead of partially parsing.
- `openclaw models scan --max-candidates 1.5` and `--concurrency 2.5` now error before scanning.

## Diagram (if applicable)

N/A

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: CLI validation is stricter for malformed inputs; regressions cover the rejected paths and no credential/auth precedence changed.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Blacksmith Testbox Linux
- Runtime/container: OpenClaw CI Testbox checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Reset Testbox checkout to `origin/impact/config-command-operator-flow`.
2. Fetch/deepen `origin/main` to establish the merge base.
3. Run the targeted command/config regression tests listed above.
4. Run `pnpm check:changed` on branch head `cd39018c61f041aaa0d14b6ea3af03f4f3709d9a`.

### Expected

- Tests and changed gate pass.

### Actual

- Tests passed: 122 total.
- `pnpm check:changed` passed with `changed_count=17` and lanes `core, coreTests, docs`.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: focused command/config regression tests and `pnpm check:changed` on Blacksmith Testbox.
- Edge cases checked: invalid section names, unknown channels, malformed/fractional numeric options, invalid include JSON, invalid setup config, and root env-substitution path labels.
- What you did **not** verify: live channel/provider services.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes, except malformed CLI inputs now fail instead of being silently accepted.
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: some scripts may have accidentally relied on junk-suffixed or fractional CLI values being truncated/defaulted.
  - Mitigation: failures now include direct correction text, and valid integer inputs keep existing behavior.

commands.author: HemantSudarshan

target.title: fix(openai): mark mp3 TTS voice output compatible
target.body:
Fixes #80317.

## Summary

- Fixes OpenAI-compatible TTS voice-note compatibility for configured `responseFormat: "mp3"`.
- Reuses the shared media runtime voice-compatibility contract instead of hard-coding OpenAI response formats.
- Adds provider and speech-core regressions so provider-compatible MP3 output continues to route as Telegram voice delivery.

## Problem summary

The OpenAI speech provider only marked voice-note synthesis as voice-compatible when `responseFormat === "opus"`. That made OpenAI-compatible local TTS servers configured for MP3 produce an audio file result even though the shared media contract and Telegram send path already allow MP3 voice delivery.

## Root cause

`extensions/openai/speech-provider.ts` used a provider-local equality check for `opus`, while the rest of the TTS/channel path uses `isVoiceCompatibleAudio()` to decide whether a produced audio asset can be delivered as a voice note. Microsoft/Edge TTS already follows that shared helper path for MP3 output, so OpenAI-compatible MP3 output diverged from the existing runtime contract.

## Architectural reasoning

This keeps the behavior at the provider boundary: the OpenAI provider still reports compatibility only for a `voice-note` synthesis target, but it now asks the shared media runtime whether the selected container extension is voice-compatible. The change does not alter provider selection, gateway/session flow, async cleanup, request timing, or Telegram transport behavior. It only corrects the metadata emitted by OpenAI-compatible synthesis results so the existing speech-core and Telegram delivery contracts can do what they already support.

## Testing performed

Local proof on rebased head `46953d268ebc22f6306247ba669562d80e26c3e9`:

- `pnpm test extensions/openai/speech-provider.test.ts extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` passed: OpenAI provider 10/10, speech-core 28/28.
- `pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose -t "routes audio media to sendAudio/sendVoice based on voice compatibility"` passed.
- Local OpenAI-compatible HTTP proof using production `buildOpenAISpeechProvider()` captured `POST /v1/audio/speech` with `response_format: "mp3"`; the returned result was `outputFormat: "mp3"`, `fileExtension: ".mp3"`, and `voiceCompatible: true`.
- `pnpm format:check -- extensions/openai/speech-provider.ts extensions/openai/speech-provider.test.ts extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD`

GitHub Actions on `46953d268ebc22f6306247ba669562d80e26c3e9`:

- Workflow Sanity #201524: success
- OpenGrep PR Diff #21168: success
- CI #207236: success
- CodeQL Critical Quality #17125: success

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenAI-compatible TTS configured with `responseFormat: "mp3"` should mark voice-note synthesis output as voice-compatible so Telegram delivery can use the voice path instead of falling back to audio-file delivery.

Real environment tested: Local Windows checkout on branch `Hemant/fix-openai-tts-mp3-voice`, Node `v22.15.0`. The extra proof used the production OpenAI speech provider against a real local HTTP OpenAI-compatible speech endpoint with redacted auth.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started a local HTTP server, invoked `buildOpenAISpeechProvider().synthesize()` with `baseUrl` pointed at that server, `responseFormat: "mp3"`, and `target: "voice-note"`, then captured the outbound OpenAI-compatible request and provider result.

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "capturedRequest": {
    "method": "POST",
    "url": "/v1/audio/speech",
    "responseFormat": "mp3",
    "model": "local-compatible-tts",
    "voice": "alloy"
  },
  "outputFormat": "mp3",
  "fileExtension": ".mp3",
  "voiceCompatible": true,
  "audioBytes": 6
}
```

Observed result after fix: The production provider requests MP3 from the OpenAI-compatible speech endpoint and returns MP3 voice-note synthesis as voice-compatible. The speech-core regression keeps that provider-compatible MP3 result on Telegram voice delivery, and the Telegram send regression confirms compatible MP3 voice delivery maps to `sendVoice`.

What was not tested: I did not send a live Telegram message or call a credentialed third-party OpenAI-compatible TTS server. The local HTTP proof exercises the same production provider request/result path without secrets.

## Risk analysis

- Runtime behavior: Narrow metadata correction for OpenAI-compatible TTS results only.
- Concurrency/session/gateway flows: No changes.
- Cleanup/lifecycle handling: No changes.
- API behavior: No public config or SDK shape changes.
- Backward compatibility: Existing Opus voice-note behavior stays compatible; WAV remains non-voice-compatible through the shared helper.
- Developer ergonomics: Removes duplicated provider-local format policy and aligns OpenAI with the shared media contract already used by other providers.

## Backward compatibility

Backward compatible. Users who explicitly configured `responseFormat: "mp3"` for OpenAI-compatible TTS now get the expected voice-compatible metadata for voice-note targets. Non-voice targets and formats that are not accepted by the shared media helper keep their previous fallback behavior.
